message:
fix(io_driver): deregister retry with NotFound tolerance and persistent failure test

Hardens IoRegistration::deregister() in three ways:

1. Treats NotFound as success — if the underlying reactor reports the
   token is already gone, the registration was effectively deregistered
   and retrying would be pointless.

2. Best-effort retry on first failure — transient errors (e.g. EINTR on
   some platforms) get one more attempt before surfacing the error.

3. Returns Ok(()) on transient-then-success — previously the method
   returned the first error even when the retry succeeded; now it
   correctly reports success.

Adds an `AlwaysFailReactor` test double that fails every deregister call,
and two tests:
- `deregister_transient_error_returns_ok_after_retry`: verifies FlakyReactor
  (fail-then-succeed) now returns Ok and driver is empty.
- `deregister_persistent_error_returns_err`: verifies AlwaysFailReactor
  surfaces the original error after 2 attempts plus 1 Drop retry (3 total),
  and the driver retains the registration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
